title: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery
summary: The checked-in aha2738 XML is a repository serialization of the University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding keyed from Andrew Clark's 1905-1911 EETS edition. The encoded target includes the Part I-II title pages in XML FRONT and Clark's printed pages 1-683 in XML BODY: a commination fragment, two devotional verse pieces, a metrical Sarum calendar, and the main Middle English cartulary. It excludes the edition's final title, forewords and introduction, Additional Notes, and indexes. Clark transcribed the surviving English text in Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. B. 408, collated the main cartulary against The National Archives E 164/20, and used that Latin register selectively for corrections and English summaries where Rawl. B. 408 is defective. A complete final-bound Clark scan is cached, with the exact target at PDF pages 151-833; two distinct U-M source-scan carriers remain modeled without claiming public image access. Current catalog descriptions, mediated reproduction routes, and unresolved rights layers are kept distinct from witnesses, editions, facsimiles, encodings, and the repository artifact.
review_notes: The intellectual cataloging subject is the bounded medieval compilation; its manuscript witnesses, Clark's edition and apparatus, print-source scans, U-M encoding, and repository XML are separate lineage layers.
Clark's documentary numbers 1-903 and the XML's 897 numbered divisions are editorial and structural units within one cartulary compilation, not independent works.
Macray's catalog quotes two short incipits but does not contain a representation of the target work; its cached PDF therefore has target_work_presence absent and no work_portion.
The current Bodleian TEI's structured production bounds are normalized literally as 1467/1480 while its display wording is retained as 'After 1467–c. 1480'.
No public full facsimile of either principal manuscript witness was verified on 2026-07-16; reproduction routes are recorded without implying that a commissioned copy may be redistributed.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2738
notes: The file is 1,975,683 bytes and is pinned through the recorded CME submodule commit.
Its 714 PB elements preserve links to the U-M print-source scan sequence, but the repository artifact is XML rather than a page-image facsimile.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text AHA2738.0001.001

date_statements.label: documented OCR and encoding state
date_statements.notes: The XML identifies OCR and Level-1 encoding work in 2006 and says that no OCR correction or editing was performed.

notes: The encoded selection contains the Part I-II title pages in XML FRONT and Clark's printed target pages 1-683 in XML BODY.

It omits the final title page, three forewords and general introduction, Additional Notes beginning at printed p. 684, and indexes through p. 722, apart from blank page milestones associated with the source scans.
U-M describes its collection production as manual keying from public-domain editions; the item XML more specifically records OCR and Level-1 encoding and says the OCR was not corrected.

entities.label: Andrew Clark's EETS edition of The English Register of Godstow Nunnery
alternate_labels: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery, near Oxford, Written about 1450

bibliographic.citation: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery, near Oxford, Written about 1450, ed. Andrew Clark, 3 parts, Early English Text Society Original Series 129-130 and 142 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1905-1911).
bibliographic.title: The English Register of Godstow Nunnery, near Oxford, Written about 1450
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 129-130 and 142
bibliographic.pages: final front matter i-cxliii; edited text 1-683; Additional Notes 684; indexes 685-722

notes: Clark says he printed all documents surviving in Rawl. B. 408, while supplying English summaries from E 164/20 where Rawl leaves are missing and using the Latin register to clarify omissions and errors.
He rearranged deeds under county, parish, chronological, and other natural heads; that order is an editorial intervention rather than the order of either manuscript.
Herbert Hurst assisted by checking doubtful places and deeds against Public Record Office E 164/20 and compiling an annotated witness list for Oxford deeds.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. B. 408

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.notes: The normalized lower bound preserves the catalog TEI's literal notBefore=1467; it does not reinterpret 'After 1467' as 1468.

holding.notes: The codex reached the Bodleian with Richard Rawlinson's collection in 1755; earlier provenance includes Godstow Abbey, Thomas Ware, and the Clarendon collection.

physical_description.format: codex containing a Middle English cartulary and prefixed devotional and liturgical material
notes: Macray notes a sketch of Godstow ruins by Anthony Wood and missing leaves between ff. 29/30 and ff. 44/45; Clark reports twelve closely written leaves cut from the codex, a count not yet reconciled with Macray's two positions.

A complete reproduction target must include covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all 224 folios, the Wood sketch, and damaged or missing-leaf positions where physically reproducible.

The current official catalog describes two msItems, a Middle English cartulary and devotional verses; Clark's edition exposes the commination, devotional, calendar, and cartulary boundaries more fully.
Macray's bracketed Lydgate attribution for the calendar is not adopted by Clark or the current Bodleian catalog and remains tentative.

entities.label: The National Archives E 164/20

date_statements.label: current catalog covering dates
date_statements.notes: The current Discovery record includes later insertions and loose documents; this archival coverage range is distinct from the initial compilation date.

date_statements.label: compilation
date_statements.notes: Amt's more specific 1403-1404 date and probable attribution to Alice of Eaton are reported through a scholarly review; Clark instead attributes the 1404 compilation's charges and labor to the prioress Alice Henley.

holding.notes: Discovery C2960643 records the item as open and held at The National Archives, Kew.

notes: A complete reproduction target is the entire bound archival volume, including binding, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all written leaves, foliation and damaged surfaces where reproducible; exact foliation and dimensions remain to be verified from the item record or physical volume.
The current record describes later insertions and loose documents as rubricated and cites binding reference E 166/1/2.
This Latin register is related only to the main English cartulary component, not to the three prefixed English components.
The Latin preserves original-language charters and witness lists and supplies a comparison source for omissions, errors, and lost English leaves.

entities.label: The National Archives Discovery record C2960643 for E 164/20

bibliographic.citation: The National Archives, Discovery C2960643, E 164/20, 'Cartulary of Godstow Abbey, Oxon.', covering dates 1404-15th century.

notes: The record marks the public record open, held at Kew, and not digitised.
The complete API JSON is catalog metadata only and contains no representation of the target compilation.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Clark's final-bound edition

The final title is PDF p. 7, binding direction p. 9, forewords begin at PDF p. 15, target printed pp. 1-683 are PDF pp. 151-833, and Additional Notes begins at PDF p. 834.

notes: The XML source-copy note says Parts I and II are bound together and preserves page-image references; the exact physical carrier boundary within the 714 PB sequence remains unresolved.
This is a scan of a printed edition carrier, not manuscript photography.

entities.label: U-M CME print-source scan carrier for Clark Part III
entities.reproduction_format: linked TIFF-derived page-image sequence for the separately sourced Part III
notes: The XML source-copy note distinguishes Part III from the Parts I-II carrier. It includes the final title, forewords, text continuation, Additional Notes, and indexes in the print source, although the U-M text selection omits most of that apparatus.
The exact physical carrier boundary within the 714 PB sequence remains unresolved; this is not manuscript photography.

access.access_method: Search Digital Bodleian by exact shelfmark and use the manuscript catalog record; no public facsimile result was found.

Ask first whether a complete internal surrogate of MS. Rawl. B. 408 already exists and can be supplied. If not, commission the complete codex through Special Collections mediated copying or Imaging Services, specifying the whole-codex surface inventory rather than only target folios.
access.format: PDF reference scans or commissioned high-resolution images
notes: Request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all 224 folios, the Wood sketch, and damaged or missing-leaf positions where reproducible.
Prefer an existing complete surrogate over new imaging when it has equivalent whole-codex coverage.

Availability and any new imaging remain subject to conservation review, legal restrictions, and scanning capacity; supply and downstream reuse are subject to Imaging Services terms and any required approval.

access.access_method: Read Discovery C2960643 or download the complete current API record.

access.format: HTML catalog record and JSON API response

access.access_method: Use Discovery C2960643; its current API record explicitly reports digitised=false, and no public complete page-image surrogate was verified.

Ask first whether The National Archives can supply an existing complete surrogate of E 164/20; if not, request complete-volume digitisation from the Digitisation Centre. Use a Record Copying page check or section order only as a fallback when a whole-volume route is unavailable.
access.format: research-quality digital images or whole-document digitisation
notes: Request the complete bound volume, including binding surfaces, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every written leaf, foliation, and damaged areas where reproducible.
Prefer an existing complete surrogate or complete-volume digitisation over piecemeal page checks and section orders.

Any copying or digitisation remains subject to service approval, document condition, and applicable fees. Commissioned copies may be used for non-commercial research, private study, or education under the service terms; publication or other reuse can require Image Library permission.

local_copies.label: Complete ORA scan of Macray's Rawlinson A-B catalog
notes: The supporting catalog includes two brief target incipits at PDF p. 334, but it contains no target-work representation and therefore has no work_portion.
All 372 pages are readable and visual inspection confirms a grayscale scan. qpdf completed its integrity check with linearization-hint warnings concerning offsets and shared-object entries.

rights.component: The checked-in XML serialization, inherited text data, and markup
rights.basis: The CME repository root license uses Unlicense wording but explicitly frames its dedication and license in terms of software; it does not clearly establish coverage of inherited text, XML data, or markup.
notes: Do not extend a software license silently across the medieval text, Clark's edition, U-M encoding, or data inherited from other providers.

rights.component: The current U-M CME digital item in the United States
rights.basis: The current item labels the resource public domain and applies the RightsStatements.org No Copyright-United States designation.
notes: The designation is territorially limited to the United States and does not by itself resolve the embedded 2006 availability statement.

rights.component: The availability and possible-copyright terms embedded in the 2006 XML
rights.basis: The embedded XML statement says that where applicable the electronic text is subject to copyright and other restrictions, without identifying the covered component or rights owner.
notes: This older layer is recorded separately from the current NoC-US statement rather than silently treated as superseded.

rights.component: Clark's 1905-1911 edition, editorial text, and published typography
rights.basis: The edition was published no later than 1911, and the current U-M item designates its source material public domain in the United States.

rights.component: The physical medieval codex as an owned object
rights.basis: Ownership and custody of a physical manuscript are distinct from copyright and reproduction rights.

rights.component: Future scans or commissioned images supplied by the Bodleian
rights.basis: Mediated copies are subject to Bodleian Imaging Services Terms of Use, and permission conditions depend on intended reuse and image service.

rights.component: The Discovery catalog record and API metadata for C2960643
rights.basis: The National Archives states that Crown copyright website content is available under the Open Government Licence unless an exception is indicated; no exception is marked for this catalog record.

license.name: Open Government Licence for Public Sector Information v3.0

notes: The Open Government Licence for catalog information does not license future page images or third-party material.

rights.component: The physical archival volume as an owned object
rights.basis: Custody of the physical archival volume is separate from copyright and image-reproduction terms.

rights.component: Future digital copies or images supplied by The National Archives
rights.basis: Record-copying terms permit non-commercial research, private study, and education, while publication, internet use, exhibition, broadcast, or other reuse may require Image Library permission and can be subject to reproduction fees.

rights.component: The Internet Archive scan, PDF derivative, and scan-provider layer
rights.basis: The cached Internet Archive metadata contains no item-specific license or rights field for the modern scan derivative; the public-domain status of Clark's underlying edition is recorded separately.

rights.component: The U-M Parts I-II print-source page-image layer in the United States
rights.basis: The exact U-M item labels its material public domain and No Copyright-United States; automated access to the page-image route remained unresolved.

rights.component: The U-M Part III print-source page-image layer in the United States
rights.basis: The exact U-M item labels its material public domain and No Copyright-United States; automated access to the page-image route remained unresolved.

rights.component: The pinned Bodleian catalog TEI and descriptive metadata
rights.basis: No repository-root license covering the catalog TEI was present at the pinned medieval-mss commit; a license found only under processing/saxon cannot be generalized to the catalog data.

rights.component: Macray's 1862 catalog expression and published typography
rights.basis: The catalog was published in 1862 and Macray died in 1916; the U.S. term for the published expression and typography expired before 2026.

rights.component: The ORA repository facsimile and record to the extent covered by the repository's designation
rights.basis: The exact ORA record displays a CC0 license designation for this repository object; the underlying 1862 catalog expression is independently public domain in the United States.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Middle English cartulary passages with omissions, mistranslations, doubtful readings, or lost leaves
editorial_practices.representation: Readings, corrections, commentary, and supplied English summaries informed by Latin E 164/20
editorial_practices.description: Clark compared the main English cartulary against E 164/20 and marked or discussed corrections; where Rawl leaves were lost he supplied English summaries from the Latin register.
editorial_practices.scope: The main cartulary on printed pages 25-683 only; not the commination, devotional verses, or calendar

editorial_practices.representation: Expansion supplied in square brackets where Clark documents the intervention
editorial_practices.description: Clark's editorial statement identifies square brackets as the representation for supplied expansions of manuscript abbreviations.
editorial_practices.scope: The edited Middle English and Latin text on printed pages 1-683

editorial_practices.source_feature: Rubricated or display text in Rawl. B. 408
editorial_practices.representation: Clarendon or heavier type in the printed edition
editorial_practices.description: Clark represents manuscript rubrication typographically rather than preserving red ink as color.
editorial_practices.scope: Rubricated headings and text throughout the edited target

editorial_practices.source_feature: Clark's complete three-part edition and final-bound apparatus
editorial_practices.representation: Part I-II title pages in XML FRONT and printed target pages 1-683 in XML BODY encoded; final title, forewords, introduction, Additional Notes, and indexes omitted from the intellectual text
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding selects the bounded medieval compilation from Clark's edition while excluding most of the modern editorial frame and back matter.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying.

evidence.summary: Establishes that mediated-copying images are subject to provider use terms and that licensing or permission may be required for reproduction beyond permitted uses.

evidence.citation: William D. Macray, Catalogi codicum manuscriptorum Bibliothecae Bodleianae, Part V, fascicle 1 (Oxford, 1862), Rawl. B. 408 at cols. 665-666, complete ORA scan.
evidence.summary: Supports the 224-folio parchment codex, provenance, anonymous translator and Alice Henley dedication, prefixed content descriptions, two brief incipits, bracketed Lydgate attribution, Wood sketch, and two specifically located missing leaves. It is a supporting catalog, not a target representation.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the cached Macray catalog PDF at the Rawl. B. 408 entry.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete carrier is a grayscale page scan and that the entry consists of catalog description with two short incipits, not a textual edition of the target.

evidence.summary: Provides bibliographic metadata, file access, terms-of-use link, and the record's CC0 license designation for the repository object.

evidence.citation: University of Oxford Community History, 'Godstow Cartularies - E 164/20, 1404, TNA, London and Rawlinson MS B 408,' 1 December 2025.
evidence.locator: section identifying the two Godstow cartularies
evidence.summary: Provides current institutional corroboration of exact TNA shelfmark E 164/20 and 1404 date, Rawlinson MS B 408, and the distinction between the Latin and English documentary representations.

evidence.citation: J. R. Maddicott, review of The Latin Cartulary of Godstow Abbey, ed. Emilie Amt, English Historical Review 130.547 (2015), 1532-1533, doi:10.1093/ehr/cev262.
evidence.summary: Confirms the English and Latin books as two versions, notes that almost all Latin charters are known from the English cartulary while Latin preserves original language and witness lists, and reports Amt's argument for Alice of Eaton as probable compiler in 1403-1404.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Discovery record C2960643, E 164/20, complete API record checked 16 July 2026.
evidence.locator: citableReference; coveringDates; scopeContent; closureStatus; heldBy; digitised; note
evidence.summary: Confirms E 164/20, covering dates 1404-15th century, open status, custody at The National Archives in Kew, the Godstow cartulary with later insertions and loose rubricated documents, digitised=false, and notes citing Clark and binding E 166/1/2.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Record copying and Digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Explains the page-check and fee workflow for digital copies and directs whole-document or high-volume requests to the Digitisation Centre.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Terms and conditions for copyright.
evidence.summary: States that Crown copyright website content is licensed under the Open Government Licence unless otherwise indicated, while excluding Discovery images, image-library material, and identified third-party content from that general permission.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Terms and conditions for record copying and copyright.
evidence.summary: Allows supplied copies for non-commercial research, private study and education under stated conditions, while publication, internet use, exhibition, broadcast and other reuse may require Image Library permission and fees.

open_questions.question: Where does the 714-PB U-M page-image sequence divide between the Parts I-II and Part III print-source carriers, and are the exact page images publicly retrievable through a supported route?
next_steps: Map every PB source and page identifier against the complete Clark scan and the XML's two source-copy notes.
Ask U-M Digital Collections whether a supported page-image or source-volume download route exists and whether the observed HTTP 403 is an automation restriction or general access boundary.
Do not merge the two physical scan carriers merely because the digital text presents one item.

open_questions.question: Does a complete public digital facsimile of Bodleian MS. Rawl. B. 408 exist outside the exact Digital Bodleian search result checked here?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Special Collections whether a complete internal surrogate exists and whether it can be made public or supplied through mediated copying.
If ordering, request the complete codex surface inventory rather than only the text-bearing folios.
Recheck Digital Bodleian and the manuscript catalog before future review.

open_questions.question: Does The National Archives hold an existing complete digital surrogate of E 164/20, and what whole-volume reproduction can it supply?
next_steps: Ask the Digitisation Centre about whole-volume imaging, existing surrogates, full physical extent, and reuse terms.
Request the entire bound volume rather than only passages corresponding to Clark's collation.
Use Discovery C2960643 as the current authoritative catalog record; its API field digitised=false establishes only the absence of a linked digitised copy, not the absence of internal surrogates.

open_questions.question: When was the English cartulary translated and compiled, and does the prologue's singular 'poor brother' represent one translator or a more complex collaborative process?
next_steps: Consult current linguistic and codicological scholarship beyond Clark's analysis.
Keep the Rawl. B. 408 production date after 1467–c. 1480 separate from the translation date.
Test vocabulary and error patterns passage by passage before modeling multiple translators.

open_questions.question: How do the current U-M NoC-US designation, the embedded 2006 availability statement, and the CME repository's software-framed Unlicense apply to the encoding, data, and repository markup?
next_steps: Request a component-specific rights statement from U-M and Kyvero Press for text, XML data, markup, and repository modifications.
Do not infer worldwide public-domain status from the territorially limited NoC-US statement.
Do not extend the CME software license to inherited XML or text data without explicit confirmation.

open_questions.question: Which Clark passages were supplied, corrected, or interpreted directly from E 164/20, and how do all 897 XML documentary divisions map to Rawl. B. 408 and Clark's 1-903 sequence?
next_steps: Build a passage-level table from XML NODE and N values, printed pages, Rawl folio milestones, and Exchequer folio milestones.
Resolve absent values 541-545, 613-614, and 828 and duplicated values 143 and 827 against the print edition.
Keep documentary divisions as structural units within one intellectual cartulary item.

open_questions.question: How should Macray's two specifically located missing leaves be reconciled with Clark's statement that twelve closely written leaves had been cut from Rawl. B. 408?
next_steps: Inspect a complete folio-by-folio surrogate or the codex, including old Roman and modern Arabic foliations.
Map Clark's Rawl-MS and Exchequer-MS folio milestones around supplied passages.
Do not treat either count alone as a complete physical-loss description without codicological reconciliation.

open_questions.question: What evidence, if any, supports Macray's bracketed attribution of the metrical calendar to John Lydgate?
next_steps: Trace the attribution through Macray's sources and modern Lydgate scholarship.
Compare the Rawl. B. 408 calendar with Douce MS 322 and cataloged Lydgate calendars.
Retain anonymous authorship unless corroborating evidence is found.
